_meta.purpose: Hand-curated strategic priorities for top 30 Tier-A accounts. Used as a fallback in pre_meeting.py Section 4 when live Companies House annual report extraction fails (massive PDFs that timeout, image-only filings, etc.). All entries are paraphrased from each company's PUBLICLY published strategic priorities (latest annual report, investor presentations, or strategic update decks). Last refreshed 2026-05.
_meta.format: Each value is a list of 3 short bullet-style strings (Sara reads these aloud).

HSBC: Focus on international wholesale, markets and wealth where HSBC has competitive advantage, exiting non-core retail markets
Boost the wealth and personal banking franchise in Asia, particularly in Hong Kong and mainland China cross-border flows
Drive operational efficiency through digitisation, AI deployment in middle office, and a flatter management structure
NatWest Group: Become the bank of choice for UK families, businesses and corporates, with sharper segment focus on retail, commercial and private banking
Simplify the bank to be easier and cheaper to run, with cost-to-income ratio target below 50%
Accelerate climate and energy transition financing, having committed £100bn in climate and sustainable funding by end-2025
Lloyds Banking Group: Deliver the strategic transformation programme: grow and diversify revenues from wealth, insurance and corporate-and-institutional banking
Modernise technology and data foundations - 75% of applications targeted to be cloud-hosted
Build deeper customer relationships through the mass-affluent proposition and embedded finance partnerships
Barclays: Re-allocate £30bn of capital from the investment bank to higher-returning UK consumer, cards and corporate banking by 2026
Deliver £2bn of gross cost savings via 'simpler, better and more dynamic' operating model and structural cost programme
Expand the US consumer business and grow Barclays UK's mass-affluent and small-business segments
Aviva: Grow the capital-light UK businesses (general insurance, health, wealth, protection) - now 56% of operating profit
Defend leadership in UK life pensions and bulk purchase annuities (BPA) where competition is intensifying
Become the UK's go-to retirement and savings provider through workplace pensions and adviser distribution
Legal & General: Simplify the group around three divisions: institutional retirement, asset management and retail
Scale the £1.2tn LGIM asset management business by deepening private markets capability and global distribution
Return £5bn-plus of capital to shareholders over 2024-27 while growing core operating profit at high single digits
Phoenix Group: Grow the open-business pension and savings franchises (Standard Life, SunLife, ReAssure) at 25% of new business value
Continue to be the UK's leading consolidator of closed life-insurance books, leveraging M&A capacity
Deliver £900m of operating cash generation reliably to support the progressive dividend
Standard Chartered: Focus on Asia, Africa and Middle East corridors where the bank has cross-border franchise advantage
Grow the affluent client business (Wealth Solutions) - target $200bn AUM uplift by 2029
Drive operational efficiency to reach a cost-to-income ratio in the low-60s, including $1.5bn productivity programme
Schroders: Grow the four strategic engines: wealth management, private assets, solutions and active equity
Expand the Schroders Capital private-markets platform towards £100bn AUM through institutional and wealth distribution
Improve operational gearing - target 'mid-50s' adjusted operating margin through technology and platform consolidation
AstraZeneca: Deliver $80bn revenue ambition by 2030, anchored on oncology, biopharmaceuticals and rare disease franchises
Launch 20 new medicines by 2030 across high-margin therapy areas, with $4bn-plus annual peak-sales potential each
Build manufacturing capacity in the US and UK as global biopharma supply chains regionalise
GSK: Strengthen the specialty medicines and vaccines portfolio, with 12 launches expected by 2031 with peak-year sales over £20bn
Deliver above-50% growth in HIV and respiratory franchises (dolutegravir, Trelegy, Nucala)
Sustain R&D productivity through AI-enabled discovery and external partnerships
Haleon: Be the world's number-one consumer health company by science-led innovation across nine power brands
Grow organic revenue 4-6% annually, with adjusted operating margin expansion through productivity and pricing
Pay down debt towards 2.5x net leverage and return surplus capital via buy-backs
Bupa UK: Be the most personal, accessible health partner - investing in clinics, dental and digital primary-care apps
Grow the UK Insurance and Health Services businesses (Cromwell Hospital, dental, care services) through integrated provision
Use technology and data to deliver better health outcomes at lower cost across all customer touchpoints
NHS England: Recover core services and reduce waiting lists - especially elective surgery, A&E performance and cancer pathways
Reform health and care delivery via Integrated Care Systems, shifting capacity from acute to community
Modernise the NHS through digital transformation (NHS App, federated data platform) and workforce expansion
National Grid: Deploy the £60bn five-year capital plan (FY24-FY29) - the largest investment programme in National Grid's history
Build out UK and US electricity transmission networks to support net-zero and the energy transition
Exit non-core gas distribution to focus the portfolio on regulated electricity infrastructure
SSE: Invest £20.5bn over 2024-2027 (£10m/day) in the electricity transmission, distribution and renewables businesses
Build out offshore wind (Dogger Bank, Berwick Bank) to be the UK's largest renewables developer
Deliver Net Zero Acceleration Programme: 50% increase in renewables output, 75% in regulated electricity assets by 2027
BP: Reset strategy back towards oil and gas: lift hydrocarbon production to 2.3-2.5 million boe/day by 2030
Cut $5bn of structural costs by 2027 through portfolio reshaping and operating efficiencies
Maintain disciplined investment in transition businesses (EV charging, biofuels) but reduce growth ambition
Shell: Maintain disciplined capital allocation - $22-25bn cash capex annually, with majority directed at upstream and integrated gas
Grow LNG sales by 20-30% by 2030 versus 2022, leveraging Shell's leadership in global LNG trading
Deliver $40-45bn shareholder distributions over 2023-2025 through dividends and buy-backs
Severn Trent: Deliver the largest investment plan in the company's history - £12.9bn during the 2025-30 regulatory period (AMP8)
Cut leakage by 16% and reduce pollution incidents by 30% during AMP8
Build new water resources (Mythe and others) and upgrade wastewater treatment to meet stricter river-water standards
United Utilities: Deliver the AMP8 (2025-30) £13.7bn investment programme - 88% increase versus the prior period
Cut storm overflow spills by 60% through £3bn programme on wastewater network
Improve customer service and operational resilience to lift performance against Ofwat's outcome delivery incentives
BT Group: Complete the full-fibre Openreach build to 25 million UK premises by December 2026
Cut £3bn of annual costs by FY29 through network simplification, AI deployment and workforce reduction
Generate normalised free cash flow of £2bn by FY27, supporting deleveraging and dividend
Vodafone: Reshape the European business after the German weakness: scale through the merger with Three UK and disposals in Italy and Spain
Grow Vodafone Business and Vodacom Africa as the engines of profitable growth
Drive efficiency and customer satisfaction to restore service-revenue growth and improve EBITDAaL margins
Microsoft: Lead the AI platform shift: embed Copilot and OpenAI capabilities across Microsoft 365, Azure and Dynamics
Grow Azure as the leading cloud for AI workloads through accelerated data-centre and silicon investment
Expand the gaming franchise post-Activision and continue to grow LinkedIn as the talent platform of record
Google: Lead the next era of AI through Gemini and Search transformation, defending the Ads moat
Grow Google Cloud at premium rates through generative-AI infrastructure and Vertex AI tooling
Drive long-term operational discipline - cap headcount growth and rationalise hardware and other 'bets'
Sage Group: Win the global mid-market accounting and HR cloud opportunity with Sage Intacct and Sage Business Cloud
Reach Rule-of-40 financial profile (organic revenue growth + operating margin) over the medium term
Deliver Sage Copilot and AI-embedded products to lift productivity for SMB customers
BBC: Deliver 'A BBC for the next generation' - reform funding model, modernise content commissioning and prioritise digital
Make BBC iPlayer the primary platform for UK audiences, with new streaming-first programming approach
Invest in trusted news and global services while finding £700m of annual savings to close the funding gap
Cabinet Office: Deliver the Government's missions and growth agenda through better cross-Whitehall coordination
Modernise the civil service via the Government People Plan - smaller, more skilled, more productive workforce
Drive digital transformation of public services through the Central Digital and Data Office
FCA: Deliver the FCA's 5-year strategy: smarter regulator, supporting growth, helping consumers and fighting financial crime
Sharpen supervision through outcomes-based Consumer Duty, intelligence-led data and faster, more decisive enforcement
Adapt to crypto, AI and DeFi while keeping UK markets internationally competitive post-Edinburgh Reforms
Macmillan Cancer Support: Be there for everyone with cancer in the UK by 2030 - reach more people earlier in their cancer pathway
Drive system change in NHS cancer care through partnership funding of nurses, professionals and services
Diversify and grow income to weather cost-of-living pressure on charitable giving
BAE Systems: Capitalise on sustained higher Western defence spending - record £77bn order backlog at end-2024
Deliver the AUKUS programme (Australian nuclear submarines) and the GCAP next-gen fighter with Italy and Japan
Grow the US business (BAE Systems Inc) and electronic systems franchise organically and through M&A
